test(client): add API error scenario and rate limiting tests (#158)

* Initial plan

- Add integration tests for 500 Internal Server Error retry
- Add integration tests for 502 Bad Gateway retry
- Add connection refused retry integration test
- Add concurrent API calls test

  for configurable error injection across all endpoints

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/a1fea383-dbcd-4de5-8599-63ee76c8492e

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove unreachable return statements after t.Fatal

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/a1fea383-dbcd-4de5-8599-63ee76c8492e

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): improve test quality from PR review feedback

- Merge duplicate 500/502 retry tests into table-driven TestDoWithRetry_RetryableStatusCodes
- Use t.Fatalf instead of t.Errorf for fatal precondition checks
- Add response status code assertion in concurrent requests test
- Add end-to-end TestDoWithRetry_IntermittentFailureWithTestServer using test server
- Simplify checkGlobalOverride locking (remove TOCTOU-prone read-lock fast path)
- Fix inaccurate comments (backoff timing, rate limiter, SetIntermittentFailure docs)

* fix(test): fail loudly when test fixture file is missing in serveJSONFile

Previously serveJSONFile silently returned {status: ok} when a
fixture file was not found, causing tests to pass while validating
nothing. Now returns HTTP 500 with a descriptive error so missing or
renamed fixtures are caught immediately.

* refactor(test): use RLock fast-path and release lock before I/O in checkGlobalOverride

Address PR review feedback: use RLock to avoid serialising normal
requests, copy override state under write lock then release before
writing the HTTP response. Remove stale doc comment referencing a
read-lock fast path that no longer matched the implementation. Add
test for missing fixture error path to cover codecov gap.

* fix(ci): make e2e deploy install cert-manager first

Use deploy-with-cert-manager in both e2e workflows so deploy no
longer fails cert-manager-check on fresh Kind clusters.

* ci(workflows): upgrade actions for node24 compatibility

Bump actions/github-script from v7 to v8 and actions/setup-go from
v5 to v6 across CI/e2e/release workflows to remove Node 20 deprecation
warnings on GitHub-hosted runners.

* fix(test): address PR review comments on override middleware and tests

- Set Content-Type: application/json in checkGlobalOverride responses
- Disable rate limiter in concurrent test for true concurrency coverage
- Fix misleading "after reset" comment to "after clearing the override"

* fix(e2e): use dynamic future date for once maintenancewindow

Replace hardcoded once interval startDate with a generated UTC future date
to keep the e2e spec valid over time and aligned with webhook validation.

* ci(e2e): increase go test timeout to 45 minutes

Raise the E2E suite timeout from 20m to 45m to prevent CI failures
caused by long-running API cleanup and reconciliation waits.

* fix(e2e): poll observedGeneration to reduce status flakes

Add a shared helper that waits for non-empty observedGeneration and use
it in maintenancewindow, account/contact, monitorgroup, and
slackintegration e2e assertions to avoid one-shot timing races.

* refactor(test): move status code into serveJSONFile to prevent silent fixture failures

Callers like handleCreateMaintenanceWindow and handleCreateMonitorGroup
wrote 201 before calling serveJSONFile, so a missing fixture would still
return 201 with a garbled body instead of a clear 500 error. Add a
statusCode parameter to serveJSONFile so headers are only written after
the fixture is successfully opened.

---------

commit.message:
Add Appendix J: ASCII, ZSCII, and Unicode Tables

Complete reference appendix covering all character sets used by
the Inform 6.44 compiler, including:

- §J.1: Character representation overview and conversion pipeline
- §J.2: Full ASCII table (0x00-0x7F) with compiler notes
- §J.3: Z-machine alphabets (A0, A1, A2) and Z-char encoding
- §J.4: ZSCII character set (0-1023) range overview
- §J.5: Default ZSCII-to-Unicode table (69 entries, Latin-1)
- §J.6: Accent escape codes grouped by type
- §J.7: Alternative character set tables (ISO 8859-2 through 8859-9)
- §J.8: Text escape sequences in strings
- §J.9: Zcharacter directive (4 forms)
- §J.10: Unicode translation table in story file
- §J.11: Library case conversion (ZSCII)
- §J.12: Glulx character handling differences
- §J.13: Character set selection (-C switch)

All data verified against Inform 6.44 compiler source (chars.c,
text.c, tables.c, directs.c).

commit.message:
fix(sync): harden atomic file writes

Route internal/sync append-only updates and internal/hooks settings writes through atomic temp-file replacement so delayed Close() failures, temp-file collisions, and partial writes do not report success. Preserve target file modes, clean up temp files, and normalize empty append-only inputs so merges do not introduce stray blank lines.

Add regression coverage for Close() handling, temp-file cleanup, destination preservation, permission preservation, and empty append-only merges.

commit.message:
fix(workout): load exercises with sets/reps/weight when starting workout (#497)

When tapping "Start Workout" from Home, the ActiveWorkout screen was loading empty.
The root cause: HomeViewModel didn't call activePlanStore.setPendingWorkoutDay() before
navigating to ActiveWorkout, so ActiveWorkoutViewModel.loadPendingWorkoutDay() had no
data to populate.

Fixed both QuickStartWorkout and StartTodayWorkout events to set the pending workout day
before navigation. Now exercises load with:
- Pre-filled sets count from the plan
- Smart defaults for weight/reps from workout history
- Fallback to sensible defaults (plan reps range) when no history exists

Closes #488

The selectors in the onboarding flow were not responding to user taps.

commit.message:
Preserve dsize across layout finalization for chained `[[no_unique_address]]` (#1235)

* Add chained no_unique_address regression

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/26388c88-8f2a-4c21-842e-c6677e927452

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve chained no_unique_address issue

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/26388c88-8f2a-4c21-842e-c6677e927452

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Removed stddef.h include from test file

* Fix: update layout_data_size when adding vptr in finalize()

When has_vtable is true, total_size was incremented by 8 for the vptr
but layout_data_size was not. The new two-parameter finalizeLayoutSize()
passes layout_data_size as raw_layout_size, so the stale value caused
polymorphic structs to report an incorrect (too small) data extent.
This could allow [[no_unique_address]] tail-padding reuse to overlap
the vptr region with subsequent members.

* Preserve layout_data_size in recalculateLayout() and finalizeWithBases()

Pass the tracked layout_data_size (data extent) separately from
current_offset (object extent) so chained [[no_unique_address]]
tail-padding reuse works for structs with base classes.

* docs: document premature layout_is_complete invariant in KNOWN_ISSUES.md

commit.message:
Disambiguate elaborated type-specifier variable declarations from class/struct definitions (#1237)

* Add elaborated type specifier disambiguation for struct/class/union variable declarations

Adds looks_like_elaborated_type_variable_declaration() lookahead that disambiguates
between struct/class/union definitions (struct Foo { ... }) and elaborated type specifier
variable declarations (struct Foo f = { 1, 2 }) at both statement and top-level scope.

This fixes parsing of patterns like 'struct timespec __ts = { ... }' in <semaphore> and
similar local variable declarations in standard library headers.

commit.message:
feat: add Packer automation for VM base image build (#34)

* feat: add Packer automation for VM base image build

Add a Packer-based workflow to automate the openclaw-k3s VM base image
creation using the hyperv-iso builder and Ubuntu 24.04 autoinstall.

- infra/packer/openclaw-k3s.pkr.hcl: Packer template using hyperv-iso
  builder with Generation 2, Secure Boot, and NoCloud HTTP autoinstall
- infra/packer/http/user-data.tmpl: Ubuntu autoinstall config with SSH
  key injection placeholder (__SSH_PUBLIC_KEY__)
- infra/packer/http/meta-data: NoCloud meta-data file for subiquity
- infra/packer/build.ps1: Windows wrapper script that injects the SSH
  public key into user-data and calls packer build
- infra/hyperv/OpenClawK3sVmDeploy.psm1: PowerShell module to deploy
  the Packer-produced VHDX as a new Hyper-V VM
- infra/hyperv/Deploy-OpenClawK3sVm.ps1: Entry-point script wrapping
  Deploy-OpenClawK3sVm
- infra/hyperv/Deploy-OpenClawK3sVm.Tests.ps1: Pester tests for the
  deploy module (5 tests, all passing)
- .github/workflows/hyperv-tests.yaml: Expanded to run all *.Tests.ps1
  files under infra/hyperv/
- .github/workflows/packer-validate.yaml: CI validation for Packer
  template using packer init + validate
- .gitignore: Exclude Packer output directories and generated user-data

The build produces a pre-installed VHDX; Deploy-OpenClawK3sVm.ps1 then
creates and optionally starts the final VM from that VHDX.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add Windows host setup guide with winget and Packer workflow

Add a 'Windows host prerequisites' section covering:
- Enabling Hyper-V via PowerShell

- Building the base VHDX with infra/packer/build.ps1
- Deploying the VM with infra/hyperv/Deploy-OpenClawK3sVm.ps1

Update the runbook to reflect the Packer-first flow as the primary
path and move the legacy ISO-based approach to a secondary option.

requirements without attempting to connect or validate runtime values.
